prevalence.trend: increasing with improved diagnosis and survival

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to growth impact and chronic disease

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms plus endoscopy with biopsy; imaging for small bowel; exclude infections

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of thousands of children across EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with access to pediatric IBD specialists and biologics

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Pediatric gastroenterology services and IBD centers widely available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Steroids for flares, immunomodulators, biologics for moderate-severe disease, nutritional support
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Long-term adherence and transition to adult care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong pediatric IBD networks and access to advanced therapies
